key_findings: Path A (SQLite-first) is the correct choice for H0-H18: zero runtime dependency on external systems; instant restart recovery; observable via SQL with no special tooling; all data safe before any sink attempt

Path B (native Pandavs ingestion) is the correct long-term destination but attempting it under active 10M+ scanning without a stable local fallback creates data loss risk if Pandavs encounters any runtime error
Merged strategy: execute Path A to H18 stability gate, then add Pandavs as a PARALLEL sink on top of SQLite outbox — never replace outbox before Pandavs passes H36 gate (row count delta < 1%)
Six non-negotiable controls apply regardless of path: write-before-sink, idempotent write, no schema-breaking changes, audit log preserved, rollback path available, UNIQUE constraint on record dedup

Eight anti-patterns must be forbidden during migration: truncating outbox, disabling WAL, changing dedup key formula mid-run, connecting Pandavs before SLO-1 drill, inline sink without outbox, removing FAILED_PERMANENT entries
H0-H6 priority is WAL + chunk_queue migration + outbox creation — these are the foundation; all other tasks depend on these three being complete
H18 go-gate requires 0 duplicate chunk executions in SLO-1 drill — this is the single most important gate and is blocking for Path B entry
DB-derived monotonic metrics (from chunk_queue.record_count_produced) must replace file-count based reports — file counts are non-monotonic under partial failure and scan retries

verdict.recommended_path: MERGED (Path A first, Path B additive after H18 gate)
verdict.transition_gate: SLO-1 drill passes (0 duplicate executions) + outbox age < 2h
verdict.rollback_to: Path A indefinitely if H36 gate fails

decisions.title: Path A before Path B: SQLite-first hardening before native ingestion
decisions.summary: Local SQLite layer must be provably stable before adding Pandavs as a sink
decisions.rationale: Pandavs integration failure during active scan = records lost with no fallback if SQLite is bypassed. Path A ensures every record is in SQLite before any sink attempt. Pandavs becomes an additional write path, not a replacement.

decisions.title: Parallel dual-sink during Path B integration (not replacement)
decisions.summary: Pandavs added as secondary sink_target in sink_outbox; SQLite outbox sweep continues
decisions.rationale: Dual-sink allows immediate rollback of Pandavs integration (disable sink_target='pandavs' in outbox sweep) without losing any records. Row count comparison between the two sinks validates integration before promoting Pandavs as primary.

decisions.title: SLO-1 drill as hard gate between Path A and Path B
decisions.summary: 0 duplicate chunk executions required before any Path B work begins
decisions.rationale: Pandavs integration under a system that can still duplicate chunk execution would introduce duplicates into Pandavs' dedup layer. The lease system must be proven correct first.

decisions.title: DB-derived monotonic metrics replace file-count reporting
decisions.summary: Hourly reports derive counts from chunk_queue.record_count_produced and sink_outbox.synced_count
decisions.rationale: File counts are non-monotonic: partial scan failures, chunk retries, and artifact rotation can produce lower file counts in later windows. DB-derived counts from the outbox ledger are monotonically increasing.

non_negotiable_controls.requirement: Every record durably in SQLite before any sink attempt
Repeated sink write of same record_id = 0 additional rows
New columns only; no DROP, RENAME, type changes
Every change reversible without data loss
UNIQUE on record_id in sink_outbox and sink tables

anti_patterns.reason: No fallback if Pandavs fails with duplicates

risk_model.title: SQLite single-writer ceiling at peak ingest
risk_model.mitigation: Batch writes per chunk; WAL mode; 194 rec/sec well within SQLite ceiling

risk_model.title: Pandavs schema incompatible with raw scan output
risk_model.mitigation: T013 validates schema compatibility before T014 dual-write; abort if incompatible

risk_model.title: Stale open runs skew audit ledger
risk_model.mitigation: Stale lease detection before any ledger reporting

risk_model.title: Host runtime mismatch blocks Pandavs module
risk_model.mitigation: T013 validates runtime; maintain Path A indefinitely if T013 fails

risk_model.title: Non-monotonic hourly reports
risk_model.mitigation: DB-derived counts from chunk_queue.record_count_produced; never file system counts

risk_model.title: H6-H18 instability window from additive changes
risk_model.mitigation: All changes are additive; scanning not paused; rollback is SQLite state revert

recommended_actions.action: Execute Path A H0-H6 tasks in strict order: WAL → chunk_queue → grant_lease → sink_outbox → write integration
Do NOT begin Path B integration until H18 go-gate passes (0 duplicates in SLO-1 drill)
Deploy master status query as monitoring baseline at H1 — provides ground truth for all subsequent decisions
Run outbox sweep in dry-run mode first (log only, no sink write) to validate record format before enabling live sink writes
At H18 gate: add Pandavs as sink_target='pandavs' in sink_outbox ALONGSIDE existing sink, not replacing it
Freeze dedup key formula for the entire 36h window — no formula changes until full cycle completes and row counts validated
Configure MTTR drill at H30: kill Pandavs module; measure restart + catch-up time vs <15 min target
Replace file-count hourly reports with DB-derived counts from chunk_queue + sink_outbox immediately at H6

assumptions: A1: Active scanning must continue throughout; no scan pause longer than heartbeat interval
A2: Pandavs module available on same host or LAN (no internet dependency)
A3: SQLite WAL handles 194 records/sec without write contention
A4: ClickHouse/Neo4j credentials available for outbox sweep at H6
A5: 51-chunk run will not complete before H18
A6: All schema changes are additive; enforced by non-destructive rule
